were placed above by
Goddess ParvatT after polishing her carefully with the idea that
she might get happiness at night through her, she would bestow all
happiness and prosperity on the whole mankind.
[The Moon is one of the eight constituents of the Lord Siva's
form. She is also said to adorn the matted locks of the Lord. Here
the poesy of the astronomer asserts itself, as he gives a fine example
of poetic fancy in this verse.
The metre is
If the Moon waxes regularly in the bright fortnight. Brah-
mins and Ksatriyas as well as all the people will enjoy power, pros-
perity and happiness. If her growth is meagre, the effect will be
loss; and if it is moderate, the result too will be likewise. These
effects will have to be declared in the reverse order in the case of
the dark fortnight.
[The metre is wifsRh]
